# JOS-S46 — Capability Doc-Standard

## Purpose

Lock the shape of a Capability artifact. Every JOS Capability conforms.

## Boundary test (before authoring)

Per JOS-C46 + JOS-D0082, an artifact is a Capability ONLY IF:

- A NON-TECHNICAL stakeholder can describe it in one sentence.
- It composes ≥2 Features.
- It persists 6+ months independent of which Features implement it.

If granular (one deliverable) → Feature.
If JOS-internal mechanism → Concept or Standard.

## Author voice

- **Stakeholder language.** No engineering jargon in the description.
- **Value framing.** Capabilities describe WHAT VALUE the system delivers.
- **Composition explicit.** Name the Features that compose this Capability + the Contracts that guarantee it.

## Required frontmatter

| Field | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id: "JOS-CAP-###"` | YES | |
| `uuid: "<v4>"` | YES | |
| `title: "<Short stakeholder-readable name>"` | YES | |
| `type: capability` | YES | |
| `owner: <name>` | YES | |
| `solve_for: > <paragraph>` | YES | stakeholder framing — what value this delivers |
| `stage_label:` | YES | |
| `domain:` | YES | |
| `provenance_tier:` | YES | |
| Universal cascade fields | YES | |
| `flexibility:` | NO | capability-manifest says has_flexibility: false; omit |
| `composed_of:` (composition, per JOS-C51) | REQUIRED | `{features: [...]}` — ≥2 features |
| `guaranteed_by:` (composition, per JOS-C51) | RECOMMENDED | `{contracts: [...]}` — what Contracts make this safe |
| `references:` | OPTIONAL | |

## Required sections (body)

1. **What this capability is** — stakeholder-readable, ≤2 paragraphs.
2. **Why it matters** — value to user / venture / stakeholder.
3. **Composing Features** — list of features that compose this capability + brief why-each.
4. **Guaranteeing Contracts** — list of contracts that make this capability safe (if any).
5. **Connected Objects** — typed cross-references.

Optional sections: maturity assessment, evolution roadmap, anti-patterns.

## Body length guidance

- **Typical**: 80-250 lines (1500-3000 chars)
- **Floor**: ~60 lines — Capabilities are stakeholder-summary scale
- **Ceiling**: ~400 lines — beyond that, you're describing implementation (move to Features/Specs)

## Composition (per JOS-C51 + JOS-D0082)

```yaml
composed_of:
  features: [feature-id, feature-id, ...]   # ≥2 required
guaranteed_by:
  contracts: [JOS-K##]                       # optional but recommended
```

The reverse edges (`Feature.composes_into` / `Contract.guarantees`) should be materialized by the composition resolver.

## Anti-patterns

- **Capability with 0 or 1 Features.** Premature — fold into existing capability or wait for the 2nd feature.
- **Engineering-language description.** Rewrite for stakeholder readability before authoring.
- **No `guaranteed_by:`.** Author the guaranteeing Contract OR mark the capability assess until one exists.
- **Bundling many capabilities into one.** Split — each capability is one stakeholder-visible value bundle.

## Worked examples

- **JOS-CAP-002 Declaration Surface for Agent-Readiness** — exemplar: stakeholder-readable, composes multiple Features.

## Validation

Forthcoming Gate: every Capability declares `composed_of.features` (≥2) + the 4 required body sections.
